This workspace is meant to contain a quarto book. The book will be about Optuna, an optimization framework. The book will be structured as indicated in the [syllabus](../syllabus.md) file. Each module will be a chapter in the book, and each practical exercise will be a section within the chapter. The instructions for the practical exercises will be provided in separate markdown files, which will be linked from the main chapters.

The instructions for the practical exercises will be written in a way that they can be easily followed by readers who are new to Optuna. They will include step-by-step guides, code snippets, and explanations of the concepts being applied in the exercises. The goal is to provide a hands-on learning experience that complements the theoretical content of the chapters.

The raw contains of this book come from an AI assistant working as data science teacher. As they were provided, they're not yet polished for publication. The content will be edited and refined to ensure clarity, accuracy, and readability before being published as a quarto book. The final version of the book will be a comprehensive guide to using Optuna for optimization tasks, suitable for advanced practitioners looking to master the framework.

The audience for this book is advanced practitioners who already have a solid understanding of machine learning, Python programming, and classical hyperparameter search methods. The book aims to take their knowledge to the next level by teaching them how to use Optuna as a general optimization framework for a wide range of applications beyond just hyperparameter tuning.

Take into consideration known technical online books to mimic the required format, using the following features:

- Clear and concise explanations of concepts
- Step-by-step guides for practical exercises
- Code snippets that are easy to understand and follow
- Real-world examples and applications of Optuna
- A logical structure that builds on previous knowledge and concepts
- Prose that is engaging and accessible to the target audience
